title: vigna campestris
intro: Vigna campestris, also known as rice bean or bean sprout, is a type of legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. It is a climbing perennial vine with yellow or white flowers and brown seed pods, native to tropical Asia. Vigna campestris has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or tea, or used in herbal formulations to promote overall health and well-being, with reported benefits including improved digestion, reduced blood pressure, and enhanced immune function.
